summary:
Iteration 32 - 4 lots Marque Blanche validated GREEN. Backend pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_32.py = 9/9 PASSED in 2.10s. Frontend Playwright = all branded surfaces verified.

BACKEND (9/9): (1) GET /api/orgs/public/iad-demo (no auth) returns full branding (name, logo_url=upload.wikimedia.org/.../Logo_IAD.svg, primary_color=#EC1C24, secondary_color=#000000, tagline='Le reseau immobilier nouvelle generation', custom_subdomain='iad'). (2) GET /api/orgs/public/iad (custom_subdomain match) resolves to the same IAD org. (3) GET /api/orgs/public/{random} = 404. (4) GET /api/orgs/by-domain on preview host = {org: null} as expected (host has <3 dot-parts). (5) POST /api/admin/whitelabel/create accepts new fields {custom_subdomain, monthly_price_per_seat_eur} - returns org with custom_subdomain set, monthly_price_per_seat_eur=1900, seats_used=0, billing_status='trialing'. (6) The newly created org is immediately reachable via /api/orgs/public/{new_subdomain}. (7) GET /api/orgs/{org_id}/billing returns all 6 expected fields (seats_used, seats_max=2, seats_available=2, monthly_price_per_seat_eur=19.0, monthly_total_eur=38.0, billing_status='trialing'). (8) POST /api/orgs/{org_id}/billing/checkout (super admin) returns 200 with checkout_url containing stripe.com - super admin bypass via is_super_admin_email works as designed in _require_org_member. (9) Seats enforcement: filled 2/2 seats then created a new user and a new invite; accept-invite returned exactly 402 with French message 'Toutes les places de l'organisation sont occupees (2/2)...' as designed.

FRONTEND: /register?org=iad-demo shows data-testid=register-brand-logo with text 'IAD FRANCE DEMO POWERED BY KOLO' + IAD tagline 'Le reseau immobilier nouvelle generation' visible in body. /login?org=iad-demo shows data-testid=login-brand-logo with the same 'IAD FRANCE DEMO POWERED BY KOLO' label. Super admin login -> /app: appshell-org-logo COUNT=0 (correct - super admin has no org_id, header stays standard KOLO). Today page: all 4 task-call/task-wa/task-email/task-cal quick-action buttons present (regression OK). /kolo-admin: admin-back-btn present; 'Marque blanche' tab opens the AI wizard; advancing to step 2 (Configuration) via 'Analyser https://iadfrance.fr' renders wl-name, wl-subdomain AND wl-price testids (count=1 each) - the AI scan correctly populated name='iad', sector='immobilier', primary/secondary colors, tagline, pitch, font='Inter'. /org for super admin shows the 'no organisation' empty state (correct - he is not a member of any org), so the live OrgSpace BillingTab UI was not rendered in-browser for this user, but the testids (org-nav-billing, org-billing-tab, billing-seats-used, billing-monthly-total, billing-checkout-btn) are all present in OrgSpace.js (grep-verified) and the underlying GET /api/orgs/{id}/billing + POST .../checkout endpoints are validated 200 in pytest above.

minor.issue: Two 401 errors still appear in browser console right after login on /app - same legacy issue carried over from iter_31. Does not block UI flows.

critical_code_review_comments: Recurring (iter_28+): /app/backend/server.py is now 7448 lines - the white-label + billing block (~6014-6320) is a strong candidate to extract to a routers/whitelabel.py router; the file is becoming hard to navigate.

/api/orgs/by-domain treats hosts with <3 dot-parts as 'no org' (parts[0] only valid when 3+). This is correct for trykolo.io but means it never matches a 2-part custom host (e.g. iad.example.com would work, but iad.io would not). Document this constraint or make the logic explicit.

OrgBillingCheckoutPayload allows arbitrary success_url/cancel_url with no scheme/domain validation - a malicious org_admin could redirect Stripe callbacks anywhere. Consider whitelisting against FRONTEND_URL or validating http(s) scheme only.

monthly_price_per_seat_eur is stored in cents but the field name says 'eur' which is misleading; the WhiteLabelTab.js divides by 100 to display, but a future maintainer reading server.py could pass 19 (cents) instead of 1900. Consider renaming to monthly_price_per_seat_cents.

Seats enforcement in accept_org_invite recomputes seats_used by counting users (db.users.count_documents) but the cached field org.seats_used is updated AFTER (line 6222-6223). If two invites are accepted concurrently the race could allow seats_used to briefly exceed seats_max. Low risk for current scale but worth flagging.

success_rate.backend: 100% (9/9 - public branding by slug + by custom_subdomain + 404, by-domain null on preview, whitelabel/create with new fields, public lookup of new subdomain, billing info, billing checkout returns stripe.com URL, accept-invite 402 when seats full)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on testable surfaces (register-brand-logo + login-brand-logo with 'IAD FRANCE DEMO POWERED BY KOLO' label and IAD tagline visible; appshell-org-logo correctly absent for super admin; Today 4 quick-actions OK; /kolo-admin admin-back-btn + 'Marque blanche' wizard step 2 renders wl-name, wl-subdomain, wl-price; OrgSpace billing testids verified by source grep - cannot render BillingTab in-browser as super admin has no org_id, but the underlying APIs are pytest-validated 100%)

test_credentials: Super admin elliot.cohenpressard@trykolo.io / Psychologue75007%! still works (login + /kolo-admin + bypasses _require_org_member for billing endpoints).
seed_data_creation: Test creates 1 org 'IAD France Test E2E' with a unique slug + custom_subdomain per run (auto-cleaned via direct MongoDB delete on fixture teardown). Seats enforcement test also creates 2 TEST_filler_* users + 1 TEST_invitee_* user + 1 invite, all cleaned at end. No leftover state expected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 32 fully validates the 4 lots marque blanche: (Lot 1) branding everywhere via /api/orgs/public/{slug-or-subdomain}, (Lot 2) /register?org=slug + /login?org=slug branding via OrgContext, (Lot 3) B2B Stripe billing (seats x monthly_price_per_seat_eur) with super-admin checkout bypass, (Lot 4) custom_subdomain field on whitelabel/create + GET /api/orgs/by-domain. The pre-seeded 'iad-demo' org (custom_subdomain='iad', logo IAD wikimedia, tagline 'Le reseau immobilier nouvelle generation') was used as a real branding fixture for the /register and /login UI tests - DO NOT DELETE THIS ORG. The WhiteLabelTab UI flow is a 3-step wizard: step1 = URL scan, step2 = config (wl-subdomain + wl-price visible here), step3 = wl-preview-brand link. To re-test step2/step3 testids, you must POST a URL via 'Analyser' first to trigger the AI scan. The OrgSpace BillingTab can only be reached by an org member (not super admin) - if you need to UI-test it, accept an org_invite as a non-super-admin user. Backend test runs in 2.1s via `pytest backend/tests/test_iteration_32.py`.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The 4 lots match the user request: (Lot1) get_public_org_branding handles both slug and custom_subdomain via $or query (line 6106); register-brand-logo + login-brand-logo + appshell-org-logo all wired through OrgContext. (Lot2) /register?org=slug and /login?org=slug both consume the public branding endpoint and inject the brand logo + tagline before authentication. (Lot3) org_billing_info computes seats_used/max/available + monthly_total live, org_billing_checkout creates a real stripe.com Subscription Checkout session (no mock) with price_data + quantity=seats + metadata={org_id, type:org_b2b}, and accept_org_invite returns 402 with French message when seats_max is reached. (Lot4) get_org_by_domain inspects the Host header and looks up custom_subdomain on the first dot-part - correctly returns {org: null} on the preview host (which has only 2 dot-parts before the public-suffix and reserved sub 'app/api/www/localhost' filter). The Stripe key in backend/.env is sk_test_* so the returned checkout_url is a sandbox URL https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_...
